candidate_summary: Model continual fine-tuning catastrophic forgetting as Heinrich-event-like quasi-periodic discharges: long quiet phases punctuated by abrupt basin-wide forgetting events triggered when a representational 'grounding line' is breached, with embedding-instability precursors and self-limiting feedback (forgotten subspace stops contributing gradient), enabling pre-event replay-checkpoint scheduling.
hit_rule: hit if kw_count>=2 OR cos>=0.7 OR judge>=0.7; kw_count counts case-insensitive substring matches of content_words in title+snippet

results.title: Heinrich event (Wikipedia)
results.judge_reason: Strong source-side coverage (Heinrich events, IRD, cycle) but no engagement with catastrophic forgetting or LLM mapping.

results.title: Heinrich events triggered by ocean forcing modulated by isostatic adjustment (Nature)
results.judge_reason: Supports the precursor/feedback half of the mechanism (ocean forcing, isostatic modulation) but only one keyword hit and no LLM relevance.

results.title: Subsurface ocean warming preceded Heinrich Events (Nature Comm)
results.judge_reason: Directly evidences the precursor claim (subsurface warming before each event) but only one keyword match and no LLM-side coverage.

results.title: Context-aware Continual Pretraining for LLMs (ICLR/NeurIPS submission)
results.judge_reason: Mentions abrupt loss of prior-domain proficiency under continual pretraining but does not frame as periodic/precursor-detectable events.

results.title: Mechanistic Analysis of Catastrophic Forgetting During Continual Fine-tuning
results.judge_reason: Cited as prior art in the novelty claim; covers mechanisms but not quasi-periodicity, precursors, or self-limiting feedback framing.

results.title: Spurious Forgetting in Continual Learning of LMs
results.judge_reason: Discusses task-alignment drops vs true knowledge loss, tangential to the Heinrich-discharge framing and yields no keyword hits.

results.title: FIT: Defying Catastrophic Forgetting in Continual LLM Unlearning
results.judge_reason: Continual unlearning utility degradation is related but does not match the periodic-discharge or precursor angle of the candidate.

results.title: Mitigating Catastrophic Forgetting in Target Language Adaptation via Source-Shielded Updates
results.judge_reason: Standard catastrophic-forgetting mitigation paper; no quasi-periodic, precursor-based, or replay-scheduling framing.

verdict: Mostly miss: source-side hit (Wikipedia) and one LLM-side hit (Mechanistic Analysis 2601.18699, explicitly cited as prior art in the novelty claim). No retrieved result articulates quasi-periodic Heinrich-like forgetting discharges with precursors and self-limiting feedback, so the specific cross-domain mapping appears genuinely novel relative to this evidence set.
